content: Patient selection criteria: BMI thresholds per guidelines above; age typically 18-65 (adolescents and elderly considered case-by-case); documented failed non-surgical weight loss attempts; psychological readiness and stability; ability to comply with lifelong follow-up and supplementation. Contraindications: uncontrolled psychiatric illness, active substance abuse, inability to participate in follow-up, prohibitive surgical risk, correctedable endocrine causes of obesity not yet treated. Preoperative evaluation: comprehensive history/physical, ECG, chest X-ray, sleep study if OSA suspected, upper endoscopy (evaluate for H. pylori, hiatal hernia, esophagitis), abdominal ultrasound (gallstones), labs (CBC, CMP, lipid panel, HbA1c, TSH, vitamin levels including B12, folate, iron studies, vitamin D, PTH), pregnancy test if applicable. Risk stratification using MBSAQIP calculator. Patient education on realistic expectations, lifestyle changes required, potential complications, need for lifelong monitoring. Insurance authorization often requires 3-6 months supervised weight management program documentation.
